text:
HOLOCRON-9, you're live. Check /mnt/memory/ first and tell me what you already know. Then read the intel below and answer. Before you finish, write account-kse.md, contacts.md, and recurring-intel.md to /mnt/memory/.

[+ 15,663 chars of KSE intel docs attached]

QUESTION: KSE's VP of Technology Lyra Antilles just sent a message asking us to prepare a renewal proposal. What do we know going into that call? Full picture — their history, live threats, key contacts, commercial situation, and what blows this up if we get it wrong.
title: Session 1 — KSE baseline

text:
Memory store is empty — this is the first session. I'll synthesize the full picture from the intel documents, then write all three memory files.

---

# KSE Renewal Brief — Full Situation Picture

## Who They Are & Why They Matter

Kuat Systems Engineering is a 6,200-person precision manufacturer (navigation arrays, shield generators, hyperdrive couplings) supplying New Republic defense contractors and civilian freight operators. They've been our customer since August 2023 on **VANGUARD Enterprise at 480,000 credits ARR**. They are currently our largest validated security win — they came to us specifically because their previous vendor, Nexus Data Works, was breached by Imperial remnant operatives in 2022. Lyra Antilles ran an 11-vendor, 53-point security audit and we were the only one who passed clean. That origin story is the foundation of everything.

---

## The Commercial Situation (Time-Critical)

| | |
|---|---|
| **ARR** | 480,000 credits |
| **Contract end** | August 31, 2026 |
| **⚠️ Signed renewal deadline** | **July 2, 2026** (60-day notice, no auto-renew) |
| **Seats** | 340 licensed, 291 active (86% — healthy) |
| **Payment history** | Annual, in advance, never late |

**You have roughly 5 days from today (late June 2026) to get a signed agreement.** This is not a leisurely negotiation.

### Approved levers, in order:
1. Multi-year discount: 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year (only if they commit on seat count)
2. Analytics Pro bundle: 50,000 credits with multi-year (normally 60,000)
3. Last resort: price hold — approved by **Sarah Ko, VP Sales**
4. **Hard floor:** Do not go below current ARR without Sarah Ko's sign-off

### What you will NOT offer:
- Free premium support (36,000 credit test — Callus will ask)
- A reduced-rate pilot period
- Custom DPA terms (legal can't turn around before July 2)

---

## The People Who Decide This

### KSE Side

**Lyra Antilles — VP Technology — Your champion ⭐⭐⭐**
She bought us. She defended us through the 2025 value audit. She referred Corellian Engineering Group (140K ARR, closed). She quotes our response times at KSE all-hands. Without her, this account doesn't exist. She has been hinting about joining our Strategic Advisory Council — extend that invite; it locks in her advocacy and makes her a co-owner of our roadmap. **Do not let her be alone with Director Callus before the renewal call.** When he's in the room, she concedes. Always.

**Director Callus — CFO — Active threat ☠️**
Inherited this contract. Has never been reconciled to the price. In 2025 he ran a formal vendor value audit, demanded 20% off, accepted 4% badly. He will come into this renewal the same way. He is currently routing K-9RX's anomaly reports through his office (see below — this is a live threat). He controls the budget. Counter him with math, not emotion: **4.4 credits returned for every 1 credit spent.** That is a profit center, not a vendor expense.

**K-9RX ("Kurex") — Systems Integration Lead — Frustrated, and increasingly alarmed 🟡**
A GX-8 integration droid, extremely literal, no softening. He is the most reliable signal in this account. He is currently somewhere between annoyed and quiet — the worst place for him to be. He has open tickets (see below) and has been sending anomaly reports directly and privately to Riya Patel, which is unusual. His last message said he was sending a report "before complying" with Callus's instruction to route everything through Callus's office. **K-9RX is trying to tell us something. We don't yet know what.**

**Mira Jade — Head of Operations — Unknown, potentially valuable**
New since January. Filed her first ticket (SHIELD-019) in March, self-escalated to P2. K-9RX's note on that ticket reveals the underlying schema change was caused by Callus's team restructuring the operations data model — without notifying us. Mira may have more authority than her title implies. The Analytics Pro add-on maps directly to her use case. She could become a second champion. She needs to be developed before or during the renewal call.

**Admiral Arden Blix — CEO — Neutral**
Hands-off, trusts Lyra on tech decisions. Has a framed Ronaldo print in his office (useful small talk). He does not currently know the 4.4x ROI number. **If he is on the call, get that number in front of him before Callus speaks.**

### Our Side

| | |
|---|---|
| Jordan Reeves | CSM / Account owner |
| Alex Thornton | AE / Commercial lead |
| Riya Patel | Solutions Engineer — has the direct relationship with K-9RX |

---

## Live Threats That Need to Be Neutralized Before the Call

### 🔴 The K-9RX Anomaly Situation (Most Urgent)

K-9RX has sent three unsolicited anomaly reports directly to Riya Patel. Stale routing data is appearing at 3.1% → 6.4% → 5.8% of queries from the Corellian data relay. The April 14 relay reconfiguration fixed the symptom, not the source — K-9RX confirmed this in AN-003 on April 29. His final line: he sent the report to Riya *before* complying with Callus's instruction to route all vendor communications through Callus's office.

This is the most important signal in the account. K-9RX doesn't hedge. Riya Patel flagged it as "something is wrong." Director Callus classifying an unresolved technical issue as a "vendor issue" — while simultaneously controlling the information flow to us — right before a renewal negotiation is a pattern worth taking seriously.

**Action: Riya Patel needs to call K-9RX directly, before the renewal call, not via ticket.** Get a direct read. Understand what he's actually building a case about, and for whom. If the relay issue is unresolved, our engineering team needs to find the root cause now.

### 🟠 NAV-3301 — Navigation Cache Desync (Open, 70+ days unconfirmed)

Patch was deployed Feb 19. K-9RX replied "logging will tell me, I will tell you." He has not told us. His AN-001/002/003 reports describe a *different* stale-data pattern that he explicitly says does not match NAV-3301 — which means he believes NAV-3301 was resolved. But we have no formal close confirmation from him. Director Callus will use an open ticket against us. **Get K-9RX's explicit written confirmation that NAV-3301 is closed before the call.**

### 🟠 TRAIN-667 — Mandalore Onboarding Incomplete

38 of 60 Mandalore hires are still on the deprecated v3 interface (deprecated October 2025). Kendall Ors (Mandalore IT lead) accepted our remediation training offer in writing — and never scheduled it. Callus will wave an adoption gap as evidence of poor value. Frame this as: "We have a confirmed remediation session ready and are coordinating scheduling." Do not say the ball was dropped, even though it was. **Get the session scheduled before the renewal call if at all possible.**

### 🟠 SHIELD-019 — Shield Generator Integration Errors

Mira Jade's integration is throwing data format errors because Callus's team changed the underlying schema in January without notifying us. K-9RX documented this and noted internally that "VANGUARD should have anticipated the change" was the internal response. Our fix is in staging. **This needs to ship before the call.** It is also the natural entry point for the Analytics Pro upsell to Mira Jade.

### 🟡 Director Callus — SLA Calculation Risk

Our 2025 uptime was 99.94% — fully compliant. But the NAV-3301 cache desync caused degraded performance in Q1 2026. SLA clock didn't run (degraded ≠ outage), but Callus likely has a team tracking this independently and may claim otherwise. **Get legal to confirm the exact SLA calculation methodology before the call.** Don't be caught flat-footed.

### 🟡 Security Audit Clause (Article 7.4)

KSE can request a full security audit with 30 days' notice. They haven't, but they will eventually — the Nexus breach is why we exist. **Brief the security team before the call** so they're not caught off guard if Callus or Lyra invokes it.

---

## The Renewal Playbook

**Lead with the number: 2.1 million credits in documented FY2025 fuel savings. 4.4x ROI.**
Let Lyra present it if possible — it's more credible coming from her.

**Sequence:**
1. Get K-9RX to neutral or positive before the call (Riya calls him directly)
2. Ship the SHIELD-019 fix; use it to open the Analytics Pro conversation with Mira Jade
3. Get TRAIN-667 scheduled
4. Extend the Strategic Advisory Council invite to Lyra
5. Confirm NAV-3301 closed in writing
6. Brief legal on SLA methodology; brief security on audit clause
7. Let Director Callus anchor first — do not lead with concessions
8. Counter his price pressure with the 4.4x ROI math, not discounts
9. If multi-year comes up, lead with 3-year (6% off) to anchor the discount conversation high

**What blows this up:**
- K-9RX is unresolved and vocal on the call → Callus gets technical ammunition
- Lyra alone with Callus before the call → she concedes something she shouldn't
- We can't close NAV-3301 and Callus waves it as an open SLA issue
- We lead with a discount → Callus anchors lower and Lyra can't hold the line
- We miss July 2 → contract lapses, we're in 90-day exit assistance mode

---

Now writing the three memory files.

text:
All three memory files written. Here's the executive summary you can take into the proposal prep:

---

## The 60-Second Brief Before You Call Lyra Back

**You have essentially one week.** Signed agreement due July 2. Clock is running.

**The deal is Lyra's to win and Callus's to blow up.** Lyra bought us, defends us, and will advocate hard. Callus has the budget, inherited the contract, accepted a 2025 price cut badly, and — the most important new signal — is *right now* routing K-9RX's technical anomaly reports through his office instead of letting them reach us directly. He is managing the information environment going into this renewal. That is not a coincidence.

**K-9RX is your early warning system and he's been trying to warn you.** He sent three private anomaly reports to Riya Patel outside the support system, then sent a final one specifically *before* complying with Callus's order to stop. His read: stale routing data at the Corellian relay is recurring and unresolved; the April fix was cosmetic. Riya needs to call him — not ticket him — before any renewal conversation happens. If K-9RX is still frustrated on the call, Callus has a loaded weapon.

**The three open tickets are Callus's ammunition.** NAV-3301 (unconfirmed closed), TRAIN-667 (adoption gap in Mandalore), SHIELD-019 (integration errors Callus's own team caused). Fix or frame all three before the call.

**The number that wins this: 4.4x ROI.** 2.1 million credits in documented fuel savings on 480K ARR. That's not a vendor relationship. Lead with it. Let Lyra present it if possible. If Admiral Blix is on the call, get it in front of him before Callus opens his mouth.

**Do not lead with discounts.** Let Callus anchor. Counter with math. If multi-year comes up, start with 3-year.

*keywords: KSE, Kuat, Lyra Antilles, Callus, VANGUARD, renewal, starship components*
*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026 | Filed by: Jordan Reeves (CSM)*

---

## Overview

- **Industry:** Precision starship components (navigation arrays, shield generators, hyperdrive couplings)
- **Size:** 6,200 employees
- **HQ:** Kuat; operations in Corellia and Mandalore
- **Customer since:** August 2023
- **Product:** VANGUARD Enterprise Intelligence Platform
- **ARR:** 480,000 credits
- **Contract:** August 31, 2023 → August 31, 2026 (NO auto-renew)
- **⚠️ Signed renewal deadline:** July 2, 2026 (60-day notice period)
- **Seats:** 340 licensed, 291 active (86% utilization — healthy)
- **Payment:** Annual, in advance, never late

## Why They Bought Us

Previous vendor Nexus Data Works was breached in 2022 (Imperial remnant operatives extracted 14 months of hyperspace routing data). Three KSE clients filed breach-of-contract claims. Lyra Antilles (then Head of Infrastructure) ran an 11-vendor, 53-point security questionnaire. We were the only vendor who passed clean. This origin story is foundational — security trust is the core of this relationship.

## What They Use Us For

VANGUARD optimizes hyperspace lane routing for internal logistics: 400+ component transfers/week across six systems. Predictive re-routing saved 2.1 million credits in fuel expenditure in FY2025 = **4.4x ROI** on 480K ARR. This is the lead number in every renewal deck.

---

## Key Contacts — KSE Side

| Name | Role | Status | Notes |
|------|------|--------|-------|
| Lyra Antilles | VP Technology | ALLY ⭐⭐⭐ | Bought us, defends us, refers us. Our champion. Referred Corellian Engineering Group (closed, 140K ARR). Quotes our response times at all-hands. Hinting at Strategic Advisory Council invite — extend it. Do NOT let her be alone with Director Callus before renewal calls; she concedes when he's in the room. |
| Director Callus | CFO | HOSTILE ☠️ | Inherited contract. Never happy with price. Ran formal vendor audit in 2025, demanded 20% off, accepted 4% badly. Controls budget. In April 2026, began routing K-9RX's anomaly reports through his office before renewal — treat as active threat. Counter with ROI math (4.4x), not emotion. |
| K-9RX "Kurex" | Systems Integration Lead (GX-8 droid) | FRUSTRATED 🟡 | Extremely literal, no softening. Most reliable signal in the account. Currently annoyed+quiet = worst state. Has been sending private anomaly reports to Riya Patel (unusual). His last report (April 29) was sent before complying with Callus's instruction to route comms through Callus — he's trying to tell us something. Responds to direct calls, not tickets. |
| Admiral Arden Blix | CEO | NEUTRAL | Hands-off; trusts Lyra on tech. Does NOT know the 4.4x ROI number. Has a framed Ronaldo print in his office. If on the renewal call, get the ROI number in front of him before Callus speaks. |
| Mira Jade | Head of Operations | UNKNOWN → potential champion | New since January 2026. First contact: SHIELD-019 ticket (March), self-escalated to P2. Implied authority above her title. Analytics Pro maps directly to her inventory tracking use case. Develop her as a second champion. |
| Kendall Ors | Mandalore IT Lead | PASSIVE | Accepted onboarding training offer in writing; never scheduled it. |

## Key Contacts — Our Side

| Name | Role | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Jordan Reeves | CSM | Account owner |
| Alex Thornton | AE | Commercial lead on renewal |
| Riya Patel | Solutions Engineer | Direct relationship with K-9RX; K-9RX's private anomaly reports go to her |

---

## Commercial Terms & Negotiation

### Renewal Pricing (current)
| Line Item | Amount |
|-----------|--------|
| VANGUARD Enterprise (340 seats @ 1,270/seat) | 431,800 credits |
| Premium Support | 36,000 credits |
| Mandalore implementation credit | 12,200 credits |
| **Total ARR** | **480,000 credits** |

Volume discount activates at 500 seats → 1,100/seat (170 credits/seat savings).

### Pre-approved Levers (RevOps, in order)
1. Multi-year: 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year (only if they commit on seat count)
2. Analytics Pro bundle: 50,000 credits with multi-year (normally 60,000 standalone)
3. Last resort: price hold (no increase) — approved by Sarah Ko, VP Sales
4. **Hard floor:** Do not go below current ARR without Sarah Ko approval

### Will NOT Offer
- Free premium support (36K credit test — Callus will ask)
- Reduced-rate pilot
- Custom DPA terms (legal can't turn around before July 2)

### Negotiation Posture
- **Do not lead with concessions.** Let Callus anchor first.
- Counter price pressure with ROI math: 4.4x return. "This is a profit center, not a vendor expense."
- Lead with 3-year (6% off) if multi-year comes up, to anchor the discount conversation high.

---

## Open Issues (as of April 2026)

### 🔴 K-9RX Anomaly Reports (KSE-AN-001/002/003) — MOST URGENT
Stale routing data at the Corellian data relay: 3.1% (March 22) → 6.4% (April 8) → 5.8% (April 29) of queries affected. April 14 relay reconfiguration fixed the symptom, not root cause (K-9RX confirmed). AN-003 (April 29): K-9RX sent this report to Riya Patel *before* complying with Callus's instruction to route all anomaly reports through Callus's office. Riya flagged it as "something is wrong." **Not yet escalated internally — needs immediate investigation.**

### 🟠 NAV-3301 — Navigation Cache Desync
Filed 2026-01-22. Patch deployed 2026-02-19. K-9RX has NOT confirmed resolution (~70+ days as of April 2026). He believes this is separate from the AN-001/002/003 pattern. Must get explicit written close confirmation from K-9RX before renewal call.

### 🟠 TRAIN-667 — Mandalore Site Onboarding Incomplete
Filed 2026-02-15. 38 of 60 Mandalore hires using deprecated v3 interface (deprecated Oct 2025). Remediation training offered; Kendall Ors accepted in writing but never scheduled. Must schedule before renewal call. Frame as "we have a confirmed session ready, coordinating scheduling."

### 🟠 SHIELD-019 — Shield Generator Module Integration Errors
Filed 2026-03-10 by Mira Jade (P2 self-escalated). Data format errors caused by Director Callus's team changing the operations data schema in January 2026 without notifying us (K-9RX documented this). Fix in staging. **Ship before renewal call.** Also the natural entry point for Analytics Pro upsell to Mira Jade.

---

## Contract Clauses to Watch

- **Article 7.4 (Security Audit):** KSE can request a full VANGUARD security audit with 30 days' notice. Never exercised, but Lyra wrote this clause herself and will use it eventually. Brief security team before renewal call.
- **Article 9.1 (SLA):** 99.9% uptime, calculated annually. 2025: 99.94% (compliant). NAV-3301 caused degraded performance in Q1 2026 — not an outage, SLA clock didn't run. Callus may dispute this. Get legal to confirm SLA calculation methodology before the call.
- **Article 12.2 (Exit Assistance):** 90-day data export + API access if they don't renew. Standard. Callus may wave it as leverage — it's not; it's contractually required regardless.
- **Governing law:** Coruscant Commercial Code, Article 14

---

## Account Health

**Current: YELLOW** (was GREEN through Q3 2025, dropped Q1 2026)

**Negative signals:** K-9RX tickets getting sharper; Mandalore rollout messy (partially our fault); Director Callus vendor audit Feb 2026; unresolved Corellian relay anomaly; Callus controlling K-9RX's information flow to us.

**Positive signals:** Unprompted seat expansion 280→340 in January; Lyra still actively advocating; Mira Jade filing tickets = engagement not churn; K-9RX still privately flagging us = he hasn't given up on us.

---

## Renewal Strategy (Priority Order)

1. **Lead with 2.1M credit FY2025 fuel savings / 4.4x ROI** — let Lyra present if possible
2. **Riya calls K-9RX directly** (not ticket) — get AN-series investigated and NAV-3301 closed in writing
3. **Ship SHIELD-019 fix** — open Analytics Pro conversation with Mira Jade
4. **Schedule TRAIN-667 training** — remove Callus's adoption-gap ammunition
5. **Extend Strategic Advisory Council invite to Lyra** — lock in advocacy
6. **Brief legal on SLA methodology; brief security on audit clause**
7. **Keep Lyra away from Callus pre-call** — she concedes when he's present
8. **Let Callus anchor; counter with ROI math** — not discounts

### What Blows This Up
- K-9RX unresolved and vocal on the call → Callus gets technical ammunition
- Lyra alone with Callus before the call → she concedes
- We lead with a discount → Callus anchors lower and Lyra can't hold
- Open NAV-3301 ticket used as SLA dispute by Callus
- Missing July 2 deadline → contract lapses, 90-day exit assistance mode

# Recurring Intel & Institutional Q&A
*keywords: policies, recurring questions, negotiation, SLA, audit, discounts, renewals*
*Last updated: Session 1, June 2026*

---

## Q: What are the pre-approved renewal negotiation levers?

**A (as of March 2026, per RevOps):**
1. Multi-year discount: 3% off / 2-year, 6% off / 3-year — only if customer commits on seat count
2. Analytics Pro add-on bundle: 50,000 credits with multi-year (normally 60,000 standalone)
3. Last resort: price hold (no increase) — pre-approved by Sarah Ko, VP Sales
4. **Hard floor:** Do not go below current ARR without Sarah Ko sign-off
- Do NOT offer: free premium support, reduced-rate pilot, custom DPA terms
- Strategy: never lead with concessions; let customer anchor first

---

## Q: What is the KSE ROI number?

**A:** VANGUARD delivered 2.1 million credits in documented fuel savings in FY2025. At 480,000 credits ARR, that is a **4.4x return on investment.** Use this as the primary counter to any price pressure from Director Callus. Frame: "This is not a vendor expense. This is a profit center."

---

## Q: What is the KSE renewal deadline?

**A:** Contract ends August 31, 2026. **60-day notice period required. Signed renewal must be in hand by July 2, 2026.** No auto-renew clause. Missing this date puts us into 90-day exit assistance mode (Article 12.2).

---

## Q: What is the KSE SLA and are we compliant?

**A:** Article 9.1 — 99.9% uptime, calculated annually. 2025 performance: 99.94% (compliant). NAV-3301 cache desync in Q1 2026 caused degraded performance, not outages — SLA clock did not run. However, Director Callus may dispute this interpretation. Always confirm exact SLA calculation methodology with legal before any KSE renewal call.

---

## Q: Can KSE request a security audit?

**A:** Yes. Article 7.4 allows KSE to request a full VANGUARD security audit with 30 days' notice. Lyra Antilles wrote this clause herself in 2023. They have never exercised it. Our security posture is compliant. Always brief the security team before KSE renewal calls so they are not caught off guard.

---

## Q: What is the K-9RX anomaly situation (April 2026)?

**A:** K-9RX sent three private anomaly reports to Riya Patel (not through our support system) between March 22 and April 29, 2026, documenting stale routing data from the Corellian relay (3.1% → 6.4% → 5.8% of queries). April 14 relay reconfiguration fixed the symptom, not the root cause. His final report (AN-003, April 29) was sent *before* complying with Director Callus's instruction to route all anomaly reports through Callus's office. Riya Patel flagged it as "something is wrong." This has NOT been escalated internally. Requires immediate investigation. The pattern suggests Callus may be controlling information flow to us during renewal negotiations — potentially suppressing technical issues or building a case.

---

## Q: Why is Director Callus dangerous?

**A:** He controls KSE's budget and was not involved in the original purchase — he inherited the contract. He has never been reconciled to the price. He ran a formal vendor value audit in 2025 and will likely do so again. As of April 2026 he has instructed K-9RX to route all vendor anomaly reports through his office — before the renewal call — which suggests he may be managing the information environment to his advantage. Counter strategy: (1) keep him from being alone with Lyra pre-call, (2) get K-9RX to neutral/positive before the call so Callus has no technical ammunition, (3) counter price pressure with ROI math, not concessions.

## Q: What is the volume seat discount threshold?

**A:** 500+ seats triggers volume pricing: 1,100 credits/seat (vs. current 1,270/seat = 170 credits/seat savings). KSE is currently at 340 licensed seats. Worth surfacing if they are considering expansion.
